report_name: Germany Unmasks Key REvil and GandCrab Ransomware Suspects

threat_actors.name: REvil
threat_actors.description: A prolific Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) syndicate, believed to be the successor to GandCrab. German authorities identified key members of the group.

threat_actors.name: GandCrab
threat_actors.description: A highly successful Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation active from 2018-2019, considered the predecessor to REvil. German authorities identified key members of the group.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: REvil and GandCrab encrypted victim data as a core part of their ransomware operations to extort payment.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware groups exfiltrated victim data and threatened to leak it publicly as part of a double extortion tactic.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The groups frequently gained initial access by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing applications like RDP servers and VPN appliances.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The groups used large-scale phishing campaigns as a method for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: The actors used tools like Mimikatz to harvest credentials for privilege escalation.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The actors abused RDP for lateral movement to deploy the ransomware payload across the network.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware payload deleted backups to increase pressure on the victim to pay the ransom.

malware.name: REvil
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the REvil group, which emerged after GandCrab's supposed retirement. It was used in high-profile attacks demanding multi-million dollar ransoms.

malware.name: GandCrab
malware.description: One of the first highly successful RaaS ransomware variants, active from 2018 to 2019. It pioneered the double extortion tactic.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: Used by the ransomware groups to harvest credentials for privilege escalation.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Used by the ransomware groups for lateral movement to deploy their payload.

identities.name: Daniil Shchukin
identities.description: A Russian national, also known as 'UNKN', publicly identified by German law enforcement as a key operative in the GandCrab and REvil ransomware syndicates.

identities.name: Anatoly Kravchuk
identities.description: A Russian national publicly identified by German law enforcement as a key operative in the GandCrab and REvil ransomware syndicates.

indicators.name: Ransomware Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via Vssadmin
id: 2b50d8a8-35e6-4429-80e3-16e331284b25
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies. This is a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGrOdp9laCw2n-Cmc8j9ZHrvQ2m7SbL3hVXGln_19qgt1pcLeJ6byl3POXqR9kz1A1O5lnTLVH3OIaQF5FnIAxNGe-fQo-C-KEuVS4eJvymWtcg_pGV6H8kUJjJ3ptBcx7bqLM0c_WSb5FXCsIVY-oPtlrylJN6NK-oV_-p72lLDXnNBxc=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a technique used by REvil and GandCrab to inhibit system recovery.

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 9a7b1c3d-e4f5-4a6b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a credential dumping tool used by REvil and GandCrab.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGrOdp9laCw2n-Cmc8j9ZHrvQ2m7SbL3hVXGln_19qgt1pcLeJ6byl3POXqR9kz1A1O5lnTLVH3OIaQF5FnIAxNGe-fQo-C-KEuVS4eJvymWtcg_pGV6H8kUJjJ3ptBcx7bqLM0c_WSb5FXCsIVY-oPtlrylJN6NK-oV_-p72lLDXnNBxc=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: critical
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of mimikatz.exe, a tool used by the ransomware groups for OS credential dumping.

indicators.name: Ransomware Note Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Ransomware Note File Creation
id: f8e7d6c5-b4a3-4987-6543-210987fedcba
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of ransomware notes with a common naming pattern used by various ransomware families.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGrOdp9laCw2n-Cmc8j9ZHrvQ2m7SbL3hVXGln_19qgt1pcLeJ6byl3POXqR9kz1A1O5lnTLVH3OIaQF5FnIAxNGe-fQo-C-KEuVS4eJvymWtcg_pGV6H8kUJjJ3ptBcx7bqLM0c_WSb5FXCsIVY-oPtlrylJN6NK-oV_-p72lLDXnNBxc=
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate files that happen to match the pattern.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '-readme.txt'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of ransomware notes matching the pattern '[random_extension]-readme.txt', a common indicator of ransomware activity.
